internal_issues.description: WIN-017 detail_evidence spectroscopic-class sentence is 99 words with a 73-word parenthetical. Split into two per curmudgeon c7 suggestion. Not BAU-eligible this run (age 7.9h; monitor/decisions/open-issues.json:ISS-1998.found_at=2026-05-12T13:20Z).

suggested_patch.find: cross-validation against spectroscopic-class distance estimates (which set absolute magnitudes from stellar spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation; even if spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchored level, the dome wobble theory predicts flat amplitude regardless of which rung the calibration sits on, so the 1/d scaling falsifies the wobble at every rung) shows the same 1/d scaling, confirming that the parallax pattern is in the geometry of the data and not an artifact of the photometric calibration chain.
suggested_patch.replace: cross-validation against spectroscopic-class distance estimates, which set absolute magnitudes from stellar spectral classification rather than from a parallax-calibrated period-luminosity relation; even if spectroscopic M_V calibrations carry residual parallax dependence at the Hyades-anchor level, the dependence is second-order and the agreement is still evidence of 1/d scaling. Gaia DR3 shows the same 1/d scaling in both parallax and spectroscopic-class distances, so the dual-independence point holds regardless.
